text: Pre-rolling with a foam roller produces a small improvement in sprint performance compared to no foam rolling.
Pre-rolling with a foam roller produces a small improvement in flexibility compared to no foam rolling.
Pre-rolling has negligible effects on jump and strength performance.
Post-exercise foam rolling reduces muscle pain perception with a small-to-moderate effect.
Post-exercise foam rolling slightly attenuates exercise-induced decreases in sprint and strength performance.
Foam rollers tend to offer larger effects on strength recovery than roller massage bars or sticks.
specific_value: foam roller: +5.6%, g = 0.27 vs roller massager: -0.1%, g = -0.01

text: Foam rolling is better justified as a warm-up activity than as a recovery tool, based on the balance of evidence across performance and recovery outcomes.
